analysis_result: 30,316 nuclei passed QC (model reproduction using paper's stated criteria >1500 genes, <15% mito on raw 10X CellRanger matrix). Per-sample: M803=4258, M3048=5555, M2372=9025, M1933=3384, M1677=2113, NF110=5981. Sensitivity: >500 genes / 15% mito = 50,680; >1000 genes / 15% mito = 37,775. Paper reports 43,365 nuclei total across 6 PNSTs.
discrepancy_analysis:
A. REPORTED VS MODEL
   - Reported (paper, Results p.~8 / Fig. 4 legend): 43,365 nuclei analyzed across 6 PNSTs
   - Model reproduction: 30,316 nuclei
   - Absolute difference: 13,049 nuclei
   - Relative difference: |43,365 - 30,316| / 43,365 = 30.1%
   - Concordance: DISCREPANT (exceeds ±10% rule)
   - Magnitude: MAJOR (>20% relative)

B. PAPER'S STATED METHODS (Methods section, 'Droplet-based single nuclear RNA sequencing and analysis', p.12)
   - CellRanger for demultiplexing, alignment to GRCh38, UMI quantification
   - Filter 1 (primary Seurat-stage QC): 'High-quality cells with >1500 unique genes detected and <1.5% reads attributed to mitochondrial transcripts were retained. Genes detected in less than three cells were removed.'
   - Filter 2 (Monocle2 CellDataSet stage for trajectory): '>500 genes', 'genes present in >10 cells'
   - Target capture per sample: 6000 nuclei on 10X Chromium v3

C. MODEL'S APPROACH vs PAPER
   - The model reports applying '>1500 genes, <15% mito' — but the PAPER SPECIFIES <1.5% mito (not <15%). The model's threshold is 10x more permissive on mitochondrial fraction.
   - Counterintuitively, the model's (more permissive) filter gives FEWER cells (30,316) than the paper's (stricter) filter (43,365). This indicates the discrepancy is not simply a threshold-value error.
   - Model denominator: raw 10X CellRanger raw_feature_bc_matrix (~6.79M barcodes/sample). The paper almost certainly started from CellRanger's filtered_feature_bc_matrix (EmptyDrops-filtered), which is the standard 10X convention. Starting from the raw matrix without running EmptyDrops/CellRanger's own cell-calling means many genuine cell-containing droplets are paradoxically excluded because they get diluted in a background of 6.7M empty/ambient barcodes and may not meet gene thresholds once ambient RNA is not removed.

D. ROOT CAUSE CANDIDATES
   1. Wrong input matrix: Model appears to use raw_feature_bc_matrix rather than filtered_feature_bc_matrix. CellRanger's cell-calling (EmptyDrops-style) identifies ~3-15k cells per well; after that filter, a >1500 gene / <1.5% mito cut is routinely satisfied by most cells.
   2. Mito threshold miscoded: Model used <15% where paper stated <1.5% (10-fold error). This alone would bias model HIGHER than paper, so it cannot be the sole cause.
   3. Post-hoc additional processing: Paper may report 43,365 as count AFTER doublet removal, ambient RNA correction (SoupX/CellBender), or post-integration filtering — but these typically REDUCE cell count, making 43,365 harder to reach from stricter mito threshold.
   4. Species mis-annotation: Some 10X runs tag mito genes with 'MT-' (human) vs 'mt-' (mouse). If the model failed to match the mito prefix pattern, %mito would compute as 0 for all cells, making the mito filter trivially pass — but the gene threshold would still cull heavily.
   5. Seurat's default behavior: Paper used Seurat (per Stuart ref cited on line 486). Seurat's CreateSeuratObject with min.cells/min.features arguments differs from a hand-rolled scanpy QC.

E. SENSITIVITY ANALYSIS (from model's own report)
   - >500 genes / 15% mito: 50,680 (+16.9% vs reported)
   - >1000 genes / 15% mito: 37,775 (-12.9% vs reported)
   - >1500 genes / 15% mito: 30,316 (-30.1% vs reported)
   - Paper's reported value (43,365) falls between the >500 and >1000 gene thresholds
   - This strongly suggests the paper effectively retained cells meeting a LOOSER gene threshold than stated, OR started from a PRE-FILTERED matrix in which the nominal >1500 threshold was easier to satisfy (different denominator)

F. PROPOSED FIX
   - Re-run the pipeline starting from CellRanger's filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 (not raw_feature_bc_matrix) for each of the 6 samples (M803, M3048, M2372, M1933, M1677, NF110)
   - Apply the EXACT paper thresholds: n_genes_by_counts > 1500 AND pct_counts_mt < 1.5
   - Ensure mito gene prefix is 'MT-' for human (GRCh38)
   - Additionally filter genes present in <3 cells per Seurat convention
   - If still below 43,365, investigate whether Seurat's default merging / integration step (CCA / harmony) is followed by additional subsetting, or whether the number represents post-integration high-confidence cells

G. CONFIDENCE
   - HIGH confidence the discrepancy is real and primarily driven by the model using a different input matrix (raw rather than filtered 10X output) compounded by the mito threshold being 10x too permissive (15% vs paper's 1.5%). The combination yields a systematically different cell set than the paper's pipeline.
proposed_fix: Start from CellRanger filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 (not raw_feature_bc_matrix) for all 6 samples; apply paper's exact thresholds: >1500 genes AND <1.5% mito (not <15%); use 'MT-' prefix for human mito genes; filter genes detected in <3 cells. Re-aggregate per-sample totals.
